exploration_log.aha_moment: make_option_opts() is a const method on Formatter, so it already has access to both column_width_ and right_column_width_ — no parameter passing needed.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could also check against (right_column_width_ - option_name_length) for a tighter fit, but that requires passing the name length as a parameter.

alternatives: Add a new configurable threshold field
key_decisions.decision: Use right_column_width_ as the threshold for wrapping
key_decisions.reasoning: right_column_width_ is already the configured width for the description text; a default longer than that would definitely overflow regardless of option name length

queries.query_text: Fix make_option_opts in Formatter_inl.hpp to wrap default values longer than right_column_width_ onto a new indented line

solve_notes: Modified `make_option_opts()` in Formatter_inl.hpp to check whether the default value string exceeds `right_column_width_` (65 by default). When it does, the default is emitted on a new continuation line indented by `column_width_` spaces instead of inline, preserving alignment of the option descriptions that follow.
task_text:
When an option has a default value string longer than the column width
(e.g., a complex path or JSON string), `Formatter::make_option_opts()`
in `Formatter_inl.hpp` appends the default inline, causing misaligned
help text. Fix the formatter to wrap long default values onto a
continuation line, preserving alignment of subsequent options.
